back_translation: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy: To the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father, and in Jesus Christ our Lord
May grace and peace from God the Father, and from Jesus Christ our Lord, abide with you all
Brothers and sisters, we are obligated to thank God always because of you, as is fitting, because your faith grows abundantly, and the love of each one of you toward one another increases
Therefore, we ourselves boast about you among the churches of God, regarding your endurance and faith in the midst of all the persecution and tribulation which you are currently enduring
This is confirming evidence of God's righteous judgment, that will reckon you worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you also are suffering
For it is righteous before God to graciously repay tribulation to those who cause you tribulation
and to graciously grant relief to you who are being oppressed, together with us, at the time when our Lord Jesus will manifest Himself from heaven, together with His angels of [royal] power
in the midst of blazing flame, when He will execute punishment on those who do not know God, and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus
They will receive penalty as eternal destruction, separated from the face of the Lord and from the glory of His [royal] strength
On that day, when He will come to receive glory in His saints, and have all who have believed marvel at Him — because you also have believed our testimony

On account of this, we therefore pray on behalf of you always, that our God will reckon you worthy of [His] calling, and will bring to completion every good-desire and work of faith with [royal] power
so that the name of our Lord Jesus may receive glory in you, and you may receive glory in Him, according to the grace of our God and of the Lord Jesus Christ
